findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Credential Storage in Plaintext
findings.description: The skill writes Discord bot tokens to ~/.claude/channels/discord/.env in plaintext. While chmod 600 is applied, this is a local file that could be read by other processes or users on the system. Bot tokens are sensitive credentials that should be stored more securely.
findings.remediation: Consider using a credential manager, encrypted storage, or OS keychain instead of plaintext .env files.

findings.title: Tool Restriction Violation - Bash Write Operations
findings.description: The skill declares allowed-tools as [Read, Write, Bash(ls *), Bash(mkdir *)], but the instructions require executing Bash commands beyond the declared restrictions. Specifically, the skill needs to write files (Write tool) and create directories (mkdir), which are not covered by the declared Bash restrictions. The Bash restrictions only allow 'ls *' and 'mkdir *', but the skill also needs to read/write .env files and access.json.
findings.evidence: allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash(ls *), Bash(mkdir *)] but instructions require reading/writing .env and access.json files, and creating directories
findings.remediation: Either expand the allowed-tools to include the necessary operations, or restructure the skill to use only the declared tools.

findings.title: Capability Inflation via Keyword Baiting
findings.description: The skill description contains excessive trigger keywords ('set up', 'configure', 'how do I set this up', 'who can reach me', 'check channel status') that could cause the skill to be activated in unintended contexts. This is a form of keyword baiting that inflates the skill's perceived scope.
findings.evidence: Description includes multiple trigger phrases: 'set up the Discord channel', 'save the bot token', 'review access policy', 'how do I set this up', 'who can reach me', 'check channel status'
findings.remediation: Reduce the description to focus on the core functionality without excessive trigger keywords.

findings.title: Potential Command Injection via $ARGUMENTS
findings.description: The skill uses $ARGUMENTS directly as a bot token value. If this is passed to a shell command without proper sanitization, it could allow command injection. The token is described as a 'long base64-ish string' but there's no validation that the input is actually a valid token format before processing.
findings.evidence: Treats $ARGUMENTS as the token (trim whitespace) without validation of token format
findings.remediation: Add input validation to ensure the token matches expected Discord bot token format before processing. Use parameterized commands or safe APIs instead of shell interpolation.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools for Complete Functionality
findings.description: The skill's allowed-tools declaration is incomplete. It declares Bash(ls *) and Bash(mkdir *) but the skill also needs to read/write files (which is covered by Read/Write tools) and potentially execute other Bash commands. The restricted Bash tools may not be sufficient for the full implementation.
findings.evidence: allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash(ls *), Bash(mkdir *)] - restricted Bash tools may not cover all required operations
findings.remediation: Review the actual tool requirements and update allowed-tools to accurately reflect what the skill needs to function.
